entry.title: RL复述演讲
entry.content:
Retell Lecture

  1. In this lecture the speaker talks about 主题

  2. 讲解型/辩论型 主题

    2.1 This lecture has provided a lot of useful information.
    2.2 The speaker argues this with supporting details.

  3. According to the speaker, s/he mentions that:

  4. And the speaker has also mentioned that 第一点 .

  5. The speaker has also given an example of [1]🌰

  6. In addition, he also gives information about 第二点.

  7. According to to the inforamtion we can get from the speaker, 第三点.

  8. And it is clear that 第四点.

  9. In conclusion, this lecture is very informative.

entry.title: RA朗读原文
entry.content:
Read Aloud



150: Soil Samples [ 新题 ]

        
        
Investigators also compared those microbes with those living in 52 other soil samples taken from all around the planet. The park had organisms that also exist in deserts, frozen tundra, forests, rainforests, and prairies. Antarctica was the only area that had microbes that did not overlap with those found in Central Park. Only a small percentage of the park’s microbes were found to be already listed in databases.
连读断句失爆 

112 MBA
Exhilarating, exhausting and intense. There are just some of the words used to describe doing an MBA. Everyone’s experience of doing MBA is, of course, different through denying that it’s hard and a demanding work whichever course you do. MBA is one of the fastest growing areas of studying in the UK so that must be a sustainable benefit against form in one pain.
连读断句失爆

124: Australian English [ 04/12/19 更新 ]
Australians speak English of course. But for many tourists and even some locals, Australian English has only tenuous links with the mother tongue. Our speech is peppered with words and phrases whose arcane meanings are understood only by the native speaker. It is these colorful colloquialisms that Australian slang is yet to truly explain. 
连读断句失爆

1: Blue 
While blue is one of the most popular colors, it is one of the least appetizing. Food researchers say that when humans searched for food, they learned to avoid toxic or spoiled objects, which were often blue, black or purple. When food dyed blue is served to study subjects, they lose appetite.
连读断句失爆

3: Tesla and Edison 
Tesla’s theoretical work formed the basis of modern alternating current electric power systems. Thomas Edison promised him almost one million dollars in today’s money to undertake motor and generator improvement. However, when Tesla, the ethical Serb, asked about the money, Edison’s reported reply was”Tesla, you don’t understand our American humor.” The pair became archrivals.
连读断句失爆

4: Productive Capacity 
        
            
        
        
The core of the problem / was the immense disparity / between the country’s productive capacity / and the ability of people to consume. Great innovations_in productive techniques / during and after the war / raised the output_of industry / beyond the purchasing capacity / of U.S. farmers and wage earners.
连读断句失爆

 
5: Father [ 修订 ] 
Every morning, no matter how late he had been up, my father rose at five-thirty, went to his study, wrote for a couple of hours, made us all breakfast, read the paper with my mother, and then went back to work for the rest of the morning. Many years passed before I realized that he did this by choice, for a living, and that he was not unemployed or mentally ill.
连读断句失爆

7: Pluto 
        
            
        
        
Pluto lost_its official status / as_a planet yesterday, when the International Astronomical Union downsized the solar system / from nine to eight planets. Although there had been_a passionate debate / at the IAU General Assembly Meeting in Prague / about the definition of_a planet - and whether Pluto met the specifications - the audience greeted the decision / to exclude_it with_applause.
连读断句失爆

8: Fiscal Year 
        
            
        
        
At the beginning of each fiscal year, funds_are allocated to each State_account / in_accordance with the University’s financial plan. Funds_are allocated to each_account / by objects of expenditure. Account managers are responsible for ensuring that / adequate funds are available in the appropriate object / before initiating transactions to use the funds.
连读断句失爆

9: Lincoln 
Lincoln’s apparently radical change of mind about his war power to emancipate slaves was caused by the escalating scope of the war, which convinced him that any measure to weaken the Confederacy and strengthen the Union war effort was justifiable as a military necessity.
连读断句失爆

10: Shakespeare 
A young man from a small provincial town – a man without independent wealth, without powerful family connections and without a university education – moves to London in the late 1580’s and, in a remarkably short time, becomes the greatest playwright not of his age alone but of all time. How was this achievement of magnitude made? How does Shakespeare become Shakespeare?
连读断句失爆

012: Akimbo 
Akimbo, this must be one of the odder-looking words in the language and puzzles us in part because it doesn’t seem to have any relatives, What’s more, it is now virtually a fossil word, until recently almost invariably found in arms akimbo, a posture in which a person stands with hands on hips and elbows sharply bent outward, one signalling impatience or hostility.
连读断句失爆

13: Yellow 
Yellow is considered as the most optimistic color, yet surprisingly, people lose their tempers most often in yellow rooms and babies will cry more. The reason may be that yellow is the hardest color for eyes to take in, so it can be overpowering if overused.
连读断句失爆

14: Elephant 
        
            
        
        
The elephant_is the largest living land mammal. During evolution, its skeleton has greatly altered / from the usual mammal, designed for two main reasons. One_is to cope with the great weight / of huge grinding cheek teeth / and elongated tusk, making the skull particularly massive. The other is to support the enormous bulk / of such_a huge body.
连读断句失爆

16: Yellow Tulip 
How do we imagine the unimaginable? If we’re asked to think of an object - say, a yellow tulip – a picture immediately forms in our mind’s eye. But what if we try to imagine a concept such as the square root of negative number?
连读断句失爆

17: Grand Canyon 
Few things in the world produce such amazement as one’s first glimpse of the Grand Canyon; it took around more than 2 billion years to create this vast wonder in some places. 17 miles wide, largely through the relentless force of the Colorado River, which runs 277 miles along its length and a mile beneath its towering rims.
连读断句失爆

78: Brain Hemispheres 
The brain is divided into two hemispheres, called the left and the right hemispheres. Each hemisphere provides a different set of functions, behaviors, and controls. The right hemisphere is often called the creative side of the brain, while the left hemisphere is the logical or analytic side of the brain. The right hemisphere controls the left side of the body, and the left hemisphere controls the right side.
连读断句失爆

82: Fast Radio Burst 
First discovered in 2007, “fast radio burst” continue to defy explanation. These cosmic chirps last a thousandth of a second. The characteristics of the radio pulses suggested that they came from galaxies billions of light-years away. However, new works points to a much closer origin-flaring star within our own galaxy.
连读断句失爆

98: Incentive Pay Schemes 
If bonus or incentive pay schemes work so well for chief executive and bankers, why does everyone not get them? After all, many jobs involve making important decisions or taking risks is there anything about corporate decision and financial risks that makes these categories of work special in terms of how they need to be incentivized and rewarded?
连读断句失爆

99: Population Growth 
How quickly is the world’s population growing? In the United States and other developed countries, the current growth rate is very low. In most developing countries, the human population is growing at a rate of 3 people per second. Because of this bustling growth rate, the human population is well on its way to reaching 9 billion within lifetime.
连读断句失爆

101: Augustus 
        
            
        
        
Augustus was given the powers_of_an_absolute monarch, but he presented himself / as the preserver of republican traditions. He treated the Senate, or state council, with great respect, and was made Consul year after year. He successfully reduced the political power of the army / by retiring many soldiers, but giving them land or money / to keep their loyalty.
连读断句失爆

102: Industrial Revolution 
As to the Industrial Revolution, one cannot dispute today the fact that it has succeeded in inaugurating in a number of countries a level of mass prosperity which was undreamt of in the days preceding the Industrial Revolution. But, on the immediate impact of Industrial Revolution, there were substantial divergences among writers.
连读断句失爆

104: Diversity of Language 
The diversity of human language may be compared to the diversity of the natural world. Just as the demise of plant species reduces genetic diversity, and deprives humanity or potential medical and biological resources. So extinction of language takes with it a wealth of culture, art and knowledge.
连读断句失爆

106: Vanilla 
The uniquely scented flavor of vanilla is second only to chocolate in popularity on the world’s palate. It’s also the second most expensive spice after saffron. But highly labor-intensive cultivation methods and the plant’s temperamental life cycle and propagation mean production on a global scale is struggling to keep up with the increasing demand for the product.
连读断句失爆

108: Teacher’s instruction 
In classes, your teachers will talk about topics that you are studying. The information that they provide will be important to know when you take tests. You must be able to take good written notes from what your teacher says.
连读断句失爆

110: Private Equity 
It isn’t rare for private equity houses to hire grads fresh out of business schools, but nine times out of ten, the students who nab these jobs are the ones who had private equity experience before even starting their MBA program.
连读断句失爆

111: No ordinary book 
This book is no ordinary book and should not be read through from beginning to end. It contains many different adventures, and the path you take will depend on the choices you make along the way. The success or failure of your mission will hinge on the decisions you make, so think carefully before choosing.
连读断句失爆

113: Legal Writing 
Legal writing is usually less discursive than writing in other humanities subjects, and precision is more important than variety. Sentence structure should not be too complex; it is usually unnecessary to make extensive use of adjectives or adverbs, and consistency of terms is often required.
连读断句失爆

114: Semiconductor 
The semiconductor industry has been able to improve the performance of electronic systems for more than four decades by making ever-smaller devices. However, this approach will soon encounter both scientific and technical limits, which is why the industry is exploring a number of alternative device technologies.
连读断句失爆

115: Two Sisters 
Two sisters were at a dinner party when the conversation turned to upbringing. The elder sister started to say that her parents had been very strict and that she had been rather frightened of them. Her sister, younger by two years, interrupted in amazement. “What are you talking about?” she said, “Our parents were very lenient”.
连读断句失爆

116: Weakness 
Weakness in electronics, auto and gas station sales dragged down overall retail sales last month, but excluding those three categories, retailers enjoyed healthy increases across the board, according to government figures released Wednesday. Moreover, December sales numbers were also revised higher.
连读断句失爆

117: Japanese tea ceremony 
The Japanese tea ceremony is a ritual tour influenced by Buddhism in which green tea is prepared and served to a small group of guests in a peaceful setting. The ceremony can take as long as four hours and there are many traditional gestures that both the server and the guest must perform.
连读断句失爆

119: Russia 
Long isolated from Western Europe, Russia grew up without participating in the development like the Reformation that many Europeans taking pride in their unique culture, find dubious value. Russia is, as a result, the most unusual member of European family, if indeed it is European at all. The question is still open to debate, particularly among Russians themselves.
连读断句失爆

120: Marketing Management 
For any marketing course that requires the development of a marketing plan, such as Marketing Management, Marketing Strategy and Principles of Marketing. This is the only planning handbook that guides students through step by step creations of a customized marketing plan while offering commercial software to aid in the process.
连读断句失爆

121: 21st century 
The beginning of the twenty-first century will be remembered, not for military conflicts or political events, but for a whole new age of globalization – a ‘flattening’ of the world. The explosion of advanced technologies now means that suddenly knowledge pools and resources have connected all over the planet, leveling the playing field as never before.
连读断句失爆

122: Orientalists 
Orientalists, like many other nineteenth-century thinkers, conceive of humanity either in large collective terms or in abstract generalities. Orientalists are neither interested in nor capable of discussing individuals; instead, artificial entities predominate. It was beneath very wide labels that every possible variety of human plurality were herded, reducing it in the process to collective abstractions.
连读断句失爆

123: Fast food 
Hundreds of millions of people buy fast food every day without giving it much thought, unaware of the subtle and not so subtle ramifications of their purchases. They just grasp a hamburger and unwrap it and tossed the wrap into the bin. The whole experience is transitory and soon forgotten.
连读断句失爆

125: Statistical Information 
The provision of accurate and authoritative statistical information strengthens modern societies. It provides a basis for decisions to be made on such things as where to open schools and hospitals, how much money to spend on welfare payments and even which football players to replace at half-time.
连读断句失爆

126: Electric Car 
First-year university students have designed and built a groundbreaking electric car that recharges itself. Fifty students from the University of Sydney‘s Faculty of Engineering spent five months working together bits of plywood, foam and fiberglass to build the concept car. They developed the specifications and hand built the car. It’s a pretty radical design: a four-wheel drive with a motor in each wheel.
连读断句失爆

127: The Preparation of Abstract
The preparation of abstracts is an intellectual effort, requiring general familiarity with the subject. To bring out the salient points of an author’s argument calls for skill and experience. Consequently, a considerable amount of qualified manpower that could be used to advantage in other ways must be diverted to the task of facilitating access to information.
连读断句失爆

129: Shrimp 
Shrimp farmers used to hold animals in nursery ponds for 30 to 60 days; now they try to move them into grow-out ponds in less than 30 days. This reduces stress on the animals and dramatically increases survivals in the grow-out ponds. Many farms that abandoned nursery ponds have gone back to them, and the results have been surprisingly positive. They’re using the old, uncovered, earthen, nursery ponds.
连读断句失爆

130: Environmental Policy Course 
Along with customary classes on subjects such as finance, accounting, and marketing, today’s MBA students are enrolling on courses for environmental policy and stewardship. Indeed, more than half of business schools require a course in environmental sustainability or corporate social responsibility, according to a survey of 91 US business schools, published in October 2005.
连读断句失爆

131: Public Demand for Education 
Public demand for education has remained strong, reflecting the importance of education as a means of social progress. Aware of the added value of education to the world of work, the government continues to innovate and update the education system in order to produce a qualified and competent workforce.
连读断句失爆

132: Business School Admissions 
Business school admissions officers said the new drive to attract younger students was in part the result of a realization that they had inadvertently limited their applicant pool by requiring several years’ work experience. Talented students who might otherwise have gone to business school instead opted for a law or policy degree because they were intimidated by the expectation of work experience.
连读断句失爆

133: Online Shopping Environments 
A unique characteristic of online shopping environments is that they allow vendors to create retail interfaces with highly interactive features. One desirable form of interactivity from a consumer perspective is the implementation of sophisticated tools to assist shoppers in their purchase decisions by customizing the electronic shopping environment to their individual preferences.
连读断句失爆

134: Hazard Assessment 
A Hazard Assessment should be performed for work involving distillations of organic liquids and should thoroughly address issues relating to residual water and possible decomposition of the solvent in question, as well as the physical placement of the distillation apparatus and heating equipment to be employed.
连读断句失爆

135: Bookkeepers 
A national study into fraud by bookkeepers employed at small and medium-sized businesses has uncovered 65 instances of theft in more than five years, with more than $31 million stolen. Of the cases identified by the research, 56 involved women and nine instances involved men. However, male bookkeepers who defrauded their employers stole three times, on average, the amount that women stole.
连读断句失爆

136: Domestic Work 
Traditional divisions of domestic work are understood to persist because of the strong association of the home with femininity and paid work with masculinity - to challenge who does what in the home is arguably equivalent to challenging what it is to be a woman or a man.
连读断句失爆

137: The Only Family 
        
            
        
        
Imagine living all your life / as the only family on your street. Then, one morning, you open the front door / and discover houses all_around you. You see neighbors tending their gardens / and children walking to school. Where did all the people come from? What_if the answer turned_out to be that / they had always been there — you just hadn’t seen them?
连读断句失爆

138: Heterogeneous Students 
To work effectively with the heterogeneous student populations found in our schools, educators have the responsibility of acknowledging the cultural backgrounds of their students and the cultural setting in which the school is located in order to develop effective instructional strategies.
连读断句失爆

139: The Most Measurable Benefit 
Perhaps the most measurable benefit of the program has been the opportunity to meet in small groups. Something that is difficult to arrange such a desperate organization. Many officers would have to work together for thirty years but would not know other’s strengths and weaknesses.
连读断句失爆

140: Teenage Girls 
Teenage girls are continuing to outperform boys in English while the gender gap in achievements in math and science has almost disappeared. The figures show that last year 80% of 14-year-old girls reached at least the expected level 5 in English, compared with 65% of boys. But in math, the girls are just 1% ahead of boys, while in science the difference is 2%.
连读断句失爆

141: Black Swan 
Before the discovery of Australia, people in the old world were convinced that all swans were white, an unassailable belief as it seemed completely confirmed by empirical evidence. The sighting of the first black swan might have been an interesting surprise for a few ornithologists, but that is not where the significance of the story lies.
连读断句失爆

142: Important Values of Literature 
        
            
        
        
Certainly / one_of the important values_of literature is that / it nourishes_our emotional lives. An_effective literary work / may seem to speak directly to us / especially if we are ripe for_it. The inner life / that good writers reveal in their characters / often gives_us glimpses / of some portion_of ourselves. We can be moved to laugh, cry, tremble, dream, ponder, shriek, or rage with_a character by simply turning the page / instead_of turning our lives upside down.
连读断句失爆

143: Internal Combustion Engine 
Internal combustion engine enables the driver to decide which source of power is appropriate for the travel requirements of a given journey. Major US auto manufacturers are now developing feasible hybrid electric vehicles, and some are exploring fuel-cell technology for their electric cars.
连读断句失爆

144: Being Green 
You used to think that being green was a luxury for your company, but climate change has made you realise that you can no longer ignore it. The buzz is about becoming carbon-neutral, but where do you start? Consider your drivers. Do you want to become carbon-neutral for marketing reasons, for financial reasons, or just to save the planet?
连读断句失爆

145: The Maximum Yield of Plants 
The maximum yield of plants, determined by their genetic potential, is seldom achieved because factors such as insufficient water or nutrients, adverse climate condition, plant diseases, and insect damage will limit growth at some stage. Plants subjected to these biotic and abiotic constraints are said to be stressed.
连读断句失爆

146: A World Without Light
        
            
        
        
Have you ever pictured_a world without light? Just think / how much we rely on man-made light sources_in_our lives. Without engineers, we wouldn’t be able to live the way we do. No street lights, no TV, no computer display, no house lights. Engineers design_and build / all these things, and they also design, build and run the electricity systems / that power all these light sources.
连读断句失爆

147: Sustainable Agriculture 
Since its inception, the UN system has been working to ensure adequate food for all through sustainable agriculture. The majority of the world’s poorest people live in rural areas of developing countries. They depend on agriculture and related activities for their livelihoods. This makes them particularly vulnerable to man-made and natural influences that reduce agricultural production.
连读断句失爆

148: Recycle 
When we recycle, used materials are converted into new products, reducing the need to consume natural resources. If used materials are not recycled, new products are made by extracting fresh, raw material from the Earth, through mining and forestry. Recycling helps conserve important raw materials and protects natural habitats for the future.
连读断句失爆

149: Language appeared from nowhere
It seems that language appeared from nowhere since no other species has anything resembling human language. However, other animals do possess basic systems for perceiving and producing sounds that enable them to communicate. These systems may have been in place before the appearance of language.
连读断句失爆

 
151: Rural Population 
Thus, a country might possess a sizeable rural population, but have an economic system in which the interests of the voters were predominantly related to their incomes, not to their occupations or location; and in such a country the political system would be unlikely to include an important agrarian party.
连读断句失爆

152: Information Technology 
The emergence of information technology has created new products, processes and distribution systems. New products include the computer, the Internet and digital TV; new processes include Internet banking, automated inventory control and automated teller machines; and new distribution systems include cable and satellite TV.
连读断句失爆

153: Informative Speech 
The purpose of the informative speech is to provide interesting, useful, and unique information to your audience. By dedicating yourself to the goals of providing information and appealing to your audience, you can take a positive step toward succeeding in your efforts as an informative speaker.
连读断句失爆

154: Method of learning 
        
            
        
        
There_is no single method_of learning / that guarantees success. How we learn that / depends_on many different factors. And what works best for you / will not necessarily be the same_as the approach / used for the other students / even they study the same course. We are all unique as learners, although some patterns emerge / from any groups_of students.
连读断句失爆

155: Electronic Discourse 
Electronic discourse is one form of interactive electronic communication. In this study, we reserve the term for the two-directional texts in which one person using a keyboard writes language that appears on the sender’s monitor and is transmitted to the monitor of a recipient, who responds by a keyboard.
连读断句失爆

156: Magnetar 
The best comparison is likely a magnetar, a young neutron star with a powerful magnetic field, the researchers said. Magnetars also produce bright X-ray flares. While magnetars are thought to be young stars, the two flaring objects in this study reside near elliptical galaxies, which contain older stars. So the objects are likely too old to be magnetars, the researchers said.
连读断句失爆

157: The Training of an Actor 
The training of an actor is an intensive process which requires curiosity, courage and commitment. You will learn how to prepare for rehearsal, how to rehearse and how to use independent and proactive processes that inform you to do the best work possible for both stage and screen.
连读断句失爆

158: Modern Food Production 
Globalization has affected what we eat in ways we are only beginning to understand. Modern food production is no longer related to our biological needs but is in direct conflict with them. The relationship between diet and our fertility, our cancer, heart disease and mental illness is becoming clear. Yet much of our food is nutritionally bankrupt.
连读断句失爆

159: Examination Candidates 
The department determines whether or not the candidate has passed the examination. In cases where an appearance for the final public oral examination would constitute a substantial financial hardship for the candidate, the director of graduate studies, may recommend to the dean of the Graduate School virtual, videoconferenced examination of the candidate.
连读断句失爆

160: Companies 
Companies will want to be known not just for the financial results they generate, but equally for the imprint they leave on society as a whole. First, ensuring that their products contribute positively. Second, operating in a way that approaches a “net-neutral” impact on the natural environment. And third, cherishing their people.
连读断句失爆

161: Constellation
A constellation is an area on the celestial sphere in which a group of stars forms an imaginary outline or pattern, typically representing an animal, mythological person or creature, or an inanimate object. The origins of the earliest constellations likely go back to prehistory.
连读断句失爆

162: Global Financial Crisis
New research shows that during the global financial crisis, workers who stayed in jobs did not reduce their working hours, despite the claims that cuts in hours have led to job losses. A study found that the life of people who stayed with the same employer remained relatively unchanged.
连读断句失爆

079: Introvert and Extrovert 
        
            
        
        
Although introvert and extrovert personality types / differ from one another on various grounds, the major difference between the two / is their source_of rejuvenation. While for extroverts, this may mean interaction_or excursions / with friends and family, the same may mean reading a book / or listening to music for introverts.
连读断句失爆

难读的句
连读断句失爆
  1. as_it seemed completely confirmed by empirical evidence.
  2. but that_is not where the significance_of the story lies.
  3. it_is these colorful colloquialisms
  4. Orientalists, like many other nineteenth-century thinkers, conceive_of humanity/ either in large collective terms / or in_abstract generalities. Orientalists are neither interested_in / nor capable_of discussing individuals; instead, artificial entities predominate. Similarly, the age-old distinction between “Europe” and “Asia” / or “Occident“ and “Orient” / herds beneath very wide labels / every possible variety of human plurality, reducing it in the process / to one or two terminal collective realities.
  5. became the greatest playwright / not of his age_alone / but_of_all time. How was this_achievement_of magnitude made?
  6. And what works best for you / will not necessarily be the same_as the approach / used for the other students / even they study the same course.

entry.title: DI描述图片
entry.content:
Describe Image


通用

Introduction:

  1. 先读标题
    1.1. The following picture shows information about 标题
    1.2. This picture shows informaiton about 标题
    1.3. This is an image showing the information about 标题

  2. 转述内容
    2.1. So from this picture we can see that.
    2.2. As we can see from the iamge

  3. Intro & 没有标题:
    3.1

Conclusion:


无规律


*Lecture Hall *

  1. The following graph gives information about an auditorium.
  2. This is a very interesting picture, and it shows a number of things.
  3. According to this graph, there are seven columns of seats, which are red.
  4. Apart from that, there is a small dais standing in front of the seating area.
  5. You can see from this graph that the indoor lighting is very bright.
  6. You can also see from this graph that there is a big and white projection screen behind the dais.
  7. And last but not the least, there are six windows in the walls.
  8. In conclusion, this graph is very informative.

The image shows the information about a lecture hall. According to the image
the seating arrangement is well designed so that every member of the audience
should be able to see clearly at all times with an unobstructed sight line, lecturer
stage is in the middle of the lecture hall, equipped with whiteboard and podium.
Moreover, after analyzing the main key aspects, it can be concluded that this
image shows crucial information about the interior design of a lecture hall, which
is strongly supported by data and facts.


地图


周期图


线图


World Income Distribution and Poverty Line in 1970 and 1990

  1. These line graphs compare world income distribution for 1970 and 1990.

  2. Highlighting the percentage of people earning below the poverty line.

  3. During this 20-year period, the number of poor people remained steady at 1.4 billion.

  4. However, due to population growth, the actual percentage of the population
    living in poverty dropped from 38 to 26%.

  5. The graphs indicate that in 1990, the majority of the world’s 5.3 billion people were earning less than ten dollars per
    day.

  6. The goal is to reduce poverty to just 13% by the year 2015.

  7. This line graph shows the 标题

  8. Different Color/Shape represent the difference 标签统称

  9. 第一个点

  10. We can see that, 有数据点

  11. Overall, this line chart is very interesting and inforamative.

  12. The folowing line graph represents the trends of 标题

  13. The data between the years 年份 and 年份 shows that 对比两个标签

  14. However, 一个标签 + increased sharply from the year 年份

  15. On the other hand, 另一个标签 was mostly steady /‘stedɪ/

饼图


垂直柱图


左右柱图


流程图

  1. This folow chart shows information of 标题
  2. The 题目 is relatively complex process with 数字 steps.
  3. The process begins with 选一个点开始描述
  4. which involves 如果有二级点
  5. It ends with 结束点
  6. It also involves
  7. In conclusion, it is very interesting and very informative about the

Hotel Reservation

  1. The following graph gives information about how a hotel reservation is executed.
  2. The steps include hotel website, request receiving by email, hotel administrator. confirm reservation by phone, customer and filling out a request form.
  3. According to this graph, the first step is hotel website, which is on a computer.
  4. The second step is to the request received by hotel administrator via email.
  5. You can see from this graph that the third step is to confirm the request by phone, followed by filling out a request form as the final step.
  6. In conclusion, this graph is very informative.

Solar Panel

  1. The following graph gives information about the solar panel to heat water. It shows how the process is done.
  2. The steps include sun rays, cool water in, transparent top, water pipe, box and warm water out.
  3. According to this graph, the first step is cool water coming in through the inlet as the blue arrow shows,
  4. followed the second step, which is sun rays coming down on the transparent top of the square box.
  5. You can see from this graph that the third step is the heat energy of sun rays heating cool water in the water pipe.
  6. The final step is the warm water out from the box through the outlet, as the red arrow shows.
  7. In conclusion, this graph is very informative.

四序图

企鹅


Lunar Cycle and affect Fish Catch Rates

  1. Our attention was primarily focused on: 上下左右 and there is: 进行描述
  2. When there is the.
  3. moving to the 方向 , when the: 什么: 怎么怎么样 ，the: 什么 怎么怎么样
  4. 继续移动重复 👆🏻 3
  5. Last but not the least

Different Heights of Four Trees


Germination of Bean Plants

  1. If we start from the left hand side, we can see that at the begining there is only a seed within the soil
  2. moving to the right, we can the seed is grow up some root.
  3. next, we can see that the seed is tring to get rid from its coat.
  4. moving foward we can see that the bean is finally come out from the soil
  5. and next, the bean is grown up with some leaves.
  6. last but not the least, we can see there is a finally matured bean with tall shoot and deep root.



Reference

entry.title: PTE流程&题型
entry.content:
PTE

Pearson Test of English Academic: 培生英语语言测试

考试 71 道小题， 180分钟。

模考

官方模考卷分为A,B,C,三套

NO.难度实际考试相似度价格
A⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️$35.99
B⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️$35.99
C⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️$35.99
Platinum全部ABC$79.99

PTE 对应 IELTS 分数

SoEMark
PTE30364250586573798386
IELTS4.55.05.56.06.57.07.58.08.59.0

PTE保 6.5分 所需正确率

目标分数64分正确率重点复习每日
朗读原文RA4 / 6⭐️⭐️⭐️50
重复句子RS3 / 9⭐️⭐️⭐️100
描述图片DI3 / 6⭐️⭐️⭐️20
复述演讲RL3 / 4⭐️⭐️⭐️20
短句问答ASQ9 / 9⭐️
短文简写SWT2 / 2⭐️⭐️⭐️8
短文写作WE1 / 1⭐️⭐️8
阅读单选MCS2 / 2⭐️
阅读多选MCM2 / 2⭐️
段落排序RP2 / 2⭐️
拖词填空FIB3 / 3⭐️⭐️20
选词填空FIB5 / 5⭐️⭐️20
录音总结SST2 / 2⭐️⭐️8
听力多选MCM2 / 2⭐️
听词填空FIB2 / 2⭐️⭐️8
摘要单选HCS2 / 2⭐️
听力单选MCS2 / 2⭐️
补全对话SMW2 / 2⭐️
识别错词HIW2 / 2⭐️
听写句子WFD2 / 2⭐️⭐️⭐️50

套题顺序与分析

分节 项目 耗时 题型 简称 权重 题量 单分 评分方式 考察能力
1
口语
77-93/M
自我介绍
朗读原文 RA ❗️❗️❗️ 13% 6-7 13 部分 口语 阅读
重复句子 RS ❗️❗️❗️ 22% 10-12 13 部分 口语 听力
描述图片 DI ❗️❗️❗️ 15% 6-7 15 部分 口语
复述演讲 RL ❗️❗️❗️ 8% 3-4 15 部分 口语 听力
短句问答 ASQ ❕❕❗️ 2% 10-12 1 正误 口语 听力
写作
短文简写 SWT ❗️❗️❗️ 7% 2-3 7 部分 写作 阅读
短文写作 WE ❕❗️❗️ 4% 1-2 15 部分 阅读
2
阅读
32-40/M
阅读单选 MCS ❕❕❗️ 0% 2-3 1 正误 阅读
阅读多选 MCM ❕❕❗️ 1% 2-3 2 部分 阅读
段落排序 RP ❕❕❗️ 2% 2-3 4 部分 阅读
拖词填空 FIB ❕❗️❗️ 3% 3-4 4-5 部分 阅读
选词填空 FIB ❕❗️❗️ 4% 5-6 4-5 部分 阅读 写作
3
听力
45-57/M
录音总结 SST ❕❗️❗️ 4% 2-3 10 部分 阅读 写作
听力多选 MCM ❕❕❗️ 1% 2-3 2-3 部分 阅读 写作
听词填空 FIB ❕❗️❗️ 4% 5-6 10 部分 阅读 写作
摘要单选 HCS ❕❕❗️ 0% 2-3 1 正误 阅读 写作
听力单选 MCS ❕❕❗️ 0% 2-3 1 正误 写作
补全对话 SMW ❕❕❗️ 0% 2-3 1 正误 写作
识别错词 HIW ❕❗️❗️ 3% 2-3 4-6 部分 听力 写作
听写句子 WFD ❗️❗️❗️ 6% 3-4 7-11 部分 听力 写作


Reference:

  1. 看这里！最全PTE交叉评分机制解析
  2. pte评分标准是什么？
  3. Score Comparison vs Other Tests

entry.title: WE欠发达国家的旅游业利弊相当
entry.content:
题目：

The disadvantages of tourism in less developed countries is as great as the advantages. What is your opinion?


作答:

The controversial topic with respect to the relationship between tourism and less developed countries never fails to attract public attention. There has been a heated debate on whether the advantages of tourism in less developed countries is as great as the disadvantages. As far as I am concerned I tend to be an advocate regarding this issue. My explanation will be illustrated in the following part of this essay.

It is reasonable for me to believe that the tourism industry is of great importance due to the fact that it can bring remarkable economic growth for the less developed country. However, it can be seen that with the increasing inbound tourism more and more traditional artifacts were being damaged. Therefore, tourism can not only leads to economic growth but also causing damage to valuable artifacts.

In contrast, there are quite a few to hold the opinion that it is of less value to scale between advantages and disadvantages caused by tourism in less developed countries. This argument does not hold water at all since people must realize that it’s not worth it when sacrificing valuable artifacts in exchange for economic growth. There is a finding conducted by the researchers at the University of Cambridge that figured out aobut 75% of historical relics damaged in a large volume of tourism activities.

In a nutshell, I may safely reach the conclusion that the advantage of tourism in less developed countries is as great as the disadvantage.

Reference:

  1. 雅思写作头脑风暴：大力发展旅游业。

entry.title: CCL车祸调节
entry.content:
This dialogue is about a motor vehicle accident and takes place outside a courtroom between a woman and a barrister.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Mrs Wu, the insurance company has accepted liability for the accident which means we don’t need any witnesses to give evidence.21
M1这是什么意思？是不是意味着这场官司我一定会赢？我能拿到我想要的赔 偿金吗？What does this mean? Does it mean I will definitely win the case? Can I get the compensation I want?
E2No. They just admitted that they have responsibility to cover the damage caused by the accident, but they don’t consider the damage to be quite as serious as you do. I’d advise you to settle it out of court.39
M2我不明白。不是说我不需要证人了，那就说明我的要求是合理的。我打赢 这场官司的可能性是很大的，为什么要庭外和解呢？I don’t understand. You said I don’t need any witnesses. That means I have a good chance to win this case, why should I settle it out of court?
E3The problem is your wrist didn’t start to bother you until six months after the accident. The judge may decide your wrist injury was not caused by the accident and only award you a small sum of money for the injury to your leg.44
M3这不是真的。当救护车驾驶员把我从车里抬出来的时候，我的腿部已经受 了伤，正在出血伤得很严重。在医院接受 X 光检查时，医生发现我的手腕 也受了伤。40That is not true. When the paramedics took me from the ambulance, my leg was injured and bleeding. It was very serious. When I had an X ray in the hospital, the doctor found that my wrist was also injured.
E4Why didn’t you tell me this earlier Mrs Wu, I didn’t even know X-rays were taken. This is very important information.21
M4我怎么会知道？我压根儿忘了提它，你应该帮助我，你是律师，你应该是 知道法律在这个国家是如何运行的。它与我们国家完全不一样, 所以我一 头雾水。44How should I have known this was important? I totally forgot about it. You should help me. You are a lawyer and you should know how the legal system works here. It is totally different from China, so I have no clue at all.
E5I put forward an offer to them of $15,000 inclusive of all costs, but they didn’t agree to it. They have come back with a counter offer of only $7,500, which will leave you with approximately $1,500 clear after paying all medical expenses and legal costs.46
M5律师费和医疗费需六千澳元? 简直不可思议。这和抢劫有什么区别。人人 都想从中捞一把，我才是真正的受伤者。Medical and legal costs are $6000? This is ridiculous, it’s almost like robbery. I am the victim but everyone else is benefiting from this.
E6Please calm down. This figure is based on the evidence you have. You cannot just ask for whatever amount you want.21
M6好吧，事已至此，我还能怎么样！请你尽量帮我打赢 官司。Alright. There is nothing I can do now. Please try your best to win the case.

entry.title: CCL腰痛复诊
entry.content:
Mrs.Guo, who previously suffered from lumbago, is talking to her GP again.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning Mrs. Guo. How are you feeling? Has the pain in your lower back improved?22
M1早上好医生。我的腰还是老样子。每次咳嗽、打喷嚏都会疼，只能天天吃 止痛药。您让我去拍 X 光，又叫我去做骨密度测验，结果出来了吗？Morning. It is still the same. It kills me every time I cough or sneeze, so I have to take painkillers every day. You asked me to do an X-ray and a bone density test. Have you gotten the results?
E2Yes, I received the results three days ago. Based on the X-ray and bone density test result, you’re suffering from osteoporosis-induced fractures in the spine.25
M2骨质疏松？啊，对了，上次您提到过这个可能性的。那天回家后我儿子帮 我上网查了一下，说那是缺钙引起的。Osteoporosis? Ah yes. Last time you mentioned the possibility of this. I asked my son to do some online research for me and he said it is caused by a lack of calcium.
E3Precisely. The lack of calcium in your bones makes them weak and more susceptible to fractures. Your persistent back pain was a result of fractures in your spine.28
M3这个病该怎么治呢？我儿子让我吃钙片， 说这样能让我以后不会轻易骨 折。我现在一天吃一片，您说这有用吗？How do you treat this condition? My son asked me to take calcium supplements, saying they would prevent me from breaking my bones easily. I take one pill per day. Do you think it helps?
E4Calcium supplements can indeed help to reduce further fractures, but its effectiveness is relatively low. I will also prescribe some other medication to strengthen your bones.26
M4唉，我最近吃的药片越来越多了。那这个药怎么吃？有什么副作用？我怕 吃这么多药，虽然老毛病治好了，又引起新毛病。I’m having more and more pills. How should I take the medicine? Are there any side effects? I’m worried that the pills will solve the old problems while causing new ones.
E5You take this medication once a week on an empty stomach. You will need to avoid food or lying down for the half an hour following. It may cause reflux, so I would recommend you cease taking your anti-inflammatory medication.40
M5可是我的腰还是疼啊。扑热息痛我还能不能继续吃？或者您再给开个什么 药？But my back is still hurting. Can I continue to take paracetamol? Or can you prescribe something else?
E6You can continue to take paracetamol as long as you do not exceed 4g every 24 hours. As for your back pain, I’ll prescribe an analgesic patch for you, which does not interact with your new medication.37
M6好的， 谢谢您， 希望能把这腰疼治好， 不然这平常什么都做不了。All right, thank you. Hopefully, the back pain will go away soon, otherwise I won’t even be able to perform daily activities.
背景:
Lumbag: 腰痛 /lʌm’beɪgəʊ/ A common, painful condition affecting the lower portion of the spine (脊柱).
Low back pain is caused by injury to a muscle (strain) or ligament (sprain). Common
causes include improper lifting, poor posture, lack of regular exercise, a fracture, a (ruptured disc 腰椎间盘突出) or arthritis (关节炎).
Often, the only symptom is pain in the lower back.
People may experience:
Pain areas: in the back, muscles and bones, hip, or leg
Sensory: leg numbness or pins and needles
Also common: back joint dysfunction or muscle spasms
Most low back pain goes away on its own in two to four weeks. Physiotherapy and pain
relievers can hel . A few cases may require surgery
重点:

entry.title: CCL疗乳腺癌
entry.content:
Mrs Wu, is consulting her doctor about the breast cancer.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1我真是不明白为什么我会得乳腺癌呢？ 我一直认为癌症应该是老年人的问题。I really don’t understand why I got breast cancer. I always thought cancer is a Geriatric problem.
M1Breath cancer is very common among middle age people. There many factors that can leads to breast cancer. For example, never having given birth to a baby, and having had problem with breast feeding.乳腺癌在中年人中非常普遍。有很多因素可以导致乳腺癌。例如，从未生过孩子，并且母乳喂养有问题。
E2我还以为没什么大不了的， 我当时要是注意一点就好了，现在想来， 那些得乳腺癌的名人好像都没有生过小孩。No wonder I haven’t had any milk since I gave birth to my daughter. I thought it was no big deal. Now I come to think of it, none of those celebrities with breast cancer seem to have had children.
M2Well that’s not the only reasons, modern lifestyle, dite and environmental factors can also be risk factors. I have your X-Ray here. From these pictures we can see there is an abnormal growth in your left breast.嗯，这不是唯一的原因，现代生活方式，饮食和环境因素也可能是危险因素。我这里有你的X光片。从这些图片中我们可以看到您的左乳房有异常生长。
E3是不是把肿块切除就可以了， 我听说乳腺癌还算是治愈率比较高的癌症， 不像肝癌什么的， 得了就基本上等于判死刑了。if I have the lump removed, would I be fine? I heard that the cure rate of breast cancer is higher than others. No like liver cancer, if you get it, you will basically be sentenced to death.
M3The problem is the growth (增生), compare with the last month this lump has grow bigger. So Mastectomy (乳房切除术) will be the best option for you. Otherwise the cancer cells may invade (入侵) other tissue with in the body.问题是增生，和上个月相比这个肿块变大了。所以乳房切除术将是你最好的选择。否则癌细胞可能会侵入体内的其他组织。
E4乳房切除术？ 真的需要这样么？ 看来我的问题还很严重可是我不愿意做这个手术你知道这将影响到我的体型的。Mastectomy? Is this really necessary? It seems that my problem is still very serious, but I don’t want to do this operation. You know it will affect my body shape.
M4Well, this is the only way to cure your breast cancer I am afraid, otherwise Metastasis (转移) can cause great damage to other part of your body, and could be much difficult to control.好吧， 恐怕这是治愈乳腺癌的唯一方法，否则转移会对身体其他部位造成很大的损害，而且很难控制。
E5好的那术后需要多长时间来恢复呢？Okay, so how long can I recover after the surgery.
M5You need to have review, within 3 month of the surgery, this is to make sure there is no Metastasis in the peripheral areas.你需要在手术后3个月内进行复查，以确保没有转移到周边区域。
E6三到六个月也不是很长的时间， 我想我可以做这个手术， 那手术会安排在什么时间呢？ 我这周五有一个很重要的会议。 我想等那以后再手术。Three to six months is not a long time. I think I can have the operation. When will the operation be scheduled? I have a very important meeting this Friday and I want to wait until then。
M6That’s fine but you need fast for 24 hours before the surgery.很好，但是您需要在手术前24小时禁食。
背景:
Breast Cancer 乳腺癌: Symptoms of breast cancer include a lump(肿块) in the breast, bloody discharge (出血) from the nipple (乳头) and changes in the shape or texture of the nipple or breast.
乳腺癌的症状包括乳房内的肿块，乳头流出的血以及乳头或乳房形状或质地的改变。
Treatment depends on the stage of cancer. It may consist of chemotherapy (化疗), radiation (方疗) and surgery.
治疗取决于癌症的发展阶段。它可能包括化疗、放疗和手术。
重点:

entry.title: CCL食物中毒
entry.content:
Mr Smith is a British man working at a branch of a multinational corporation in Beijing. He has suddenly felt dizzy(晕眩的) and fatigued (疲乏), and has come to a nearby hospital, where he sees doctor Wei.

        
        
IDOriginalTranslated
M1史密斯先生你好可以简单请您简单的描述一下你的病情么？Hello Mr. Smith, could you please briefly describe your condition?
E1Well, the weather was really nice today, so I decided to go for a walk in the park. At first everything seemed fine, but an hour later I started to feel dizzy and lacked the energy to do anything.今天天气很好，所以我决定去公园散步。 刚开始好像一切都正常，但是一个小时以后我开始感觉头晕并且没力气做任何事。
M2恩，这样吧，我先给您量一下体温和血压好吗？ 嗯，36.8°，体温很正常。 低压80，高压120，也很正常啊。您最近休息得好吗？I see. I’ll check your body temperature and blood pressure now. Hmm, your temperature is 36.8° degrees Celsius., that’s very normal. Your blood pressure is 120 over 80, which is also very normal. Have you been resting well lately?
E2Yes I go to bed before 10 everynight.是的，我每天晚上都在10点之前去睡觉。
M3嗯很好最近的工作压力大么？Hmm, good. Do you feel stress from work recentely?
E3It’s been Little more hectic (忙碌), Recently I was promoted to the deputy director (副总经理). And yesterday my colleagues threw party for me to celebrate. And I tried many delicious Chinese dishes.是有一些忙碌，我最近刚被提拔为我们公司的副总经理。 而且昨天我的同事给我开了一个 庆祝晚会，我尝试了很多美味的中国菜
M4哟， 那可真要祝贺您啦。 您都吃了那些东西啊？Ah! Then I must congratulate you (contratulations on you)! What kind of things did you eat?]
E4Thank you. I was most taken with the spicy chicken dish and stir-fried tofu. I also tried mushroom, and raw fish – which I had never eaten before – in fact my co-workers had to urge me to try it. When I finally gave in, I found it most delightful (非常好吃).谢谢。我很喜欢吃辣鸡肉料理和炒豆腐。我还尝试了蘑菇和生鱼，这些都是我之前从来没吃过的。事实上是我的同事怂恿我去尝试的。我最终屈服了，还发现它非常好吃。
M5我觉得问题就出现在这里。首先，吃野生动物存在着很大的危险性； 鲜蘑菇一定要确保无毒、干净才可以吃；海鲜也最好不要生吃。I think this is where the problem started. Eating wild animals is very risky. Firstly, raw mushrooms should be checked to see if they are non-poisonous and clean before eating. As for seafood, it’s best not eaten raw.
E5Oh, I had no idea. During yesterday’s festivities (庆祝活动), I tried many dishes I didn’t even know the names for. So from now on, I will be more careful about what I eat.噢，我不知道。在昨天的庆祝活动上，我尝试了许多我连名字都叫不上来的菜。从现在开始，我将会对要入口的食物更加谨慎。
M6对，“吃一堑，长一智”嘛！这样吧，我先给您开一个药方吧。每天早中晚三次，一次三片。一个疗程后就您就会感觉好些了！You can put it down to experience / (take it as a lessons learned). OK, so I’ll write you out a prescription. Take three tablets a day, morning, noon and night. After one course you should feel much better.
E6Thank you.谢谢
重点:

entry.title: CCL经济舱症
entry.content:
A yong women is consultanting her GP about Air-Travel
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good Afternoon Miss Xu, I understand you about to go to Europ for a month long holiday.下午好，徐小姐，我了解到你要去欧洲度假一个月。
M1是的医生这也是我今天来见你的原因，最近在电视和报纸上报道了许多关于经济舱综合症的事儿，我这次到欧洲旅游要乘20多个小时的飞机， 买的就是经济舱的票， 您说我会得经济舱综合症么？Yes, doctor, that’s why I came to see you today. Recentely, there have been a lot of reports about Economic-Class-Syndrome on TV and Nespapers. It will take me over 20 hours to fly to Europe, I bought an economic ticket. Do you think I will get Economic-Class-Syndrome?
E2There are many factors that can predispose someone to developing economy class syndrome. These factors can be genetic (基因； /dʒɪ’netɪk/) or life style realted, seating still for long period of time is unfortunately one of them, which is why its ofen occurs among travellers on long distance air-flight.有很多因素会使人容易患上经济舱综合征。这些因素可能是基因或生活方式的关系，不幸的是，长时间坐着不动是其中之一，这就是为什么它经常发生在长途飞行的旅客中。
M2对， 我就是看了九号电视台的新闻时事节目， 才知道远程飞行可能会导致经济舱综合症的， 以前我从没听说过这种病也不了解所以非常担心。Yes, I just watched A current Affair on Channel Nine and realized that long-distance flight may cause Economy Class Syndrome. I have never heard of this disease before and I don’t understand it.
E3Ok, how about people who suffer from it, almost always have additional risk factors. Such as Coronary Heart Disease, smoking and family history of thrombosis or a high doses of Combined Oral Contraceptives. Do any of this apply to you?好吧，那么患有这种病的人呢， 他们几乎总是有另外的风险因素， 如冠心病，吸烟和家族遗传的血栓或高剂量的复方口服避孕药。
M3我正在服用复方口服避孕药是这个医疗中心的另一位医生给我的，不过我没有家族史， 从不吸烟， 也没有心脏病。I’m on a Combined Oral Contraceptives pill that was given to me by another doctor at this medical center, but I have no family history, never smoked, and I don’t have a heart disease.
E4The risk of you getting DVT during your flight is very low. and that risk can be further reduced if you heed a number of recommendations你在飞行中患上深度静脉血栓(Deep Vein Thrombosis)的风险非常低。如果你听从一些建议，这种风险可以进一步降低
M4要不要停止服用我的避孕药呢？ 我的朋友建议我吃阿司匹林， 说这样能减少我得经济舱综合症的风险， 这是真的吗？Should I stop taking my Combined Oral Contraceptives? My friend suggested me to take aspirin, saying it would reduce the risk of getting Economy Class Syndrome. Is this true?
E5You don’t need to cease to taking your pills as the risks a lot, and for aspirn its may cause excessive bleeding in some cases. So I would not recommend that you take it as a preventative measure.你不必停药，因为风险太大，而且阿司匹林有时会引起大出血。 所以我不建议你把它作为一种预防措施。
M5听你解释后我就放心了心了， 我平时很少坐长途飞机， 所以担心会得这种综合症， 现在我不担心了， 谢谢你医生。After hearing your explanation, I feel relieved. I don’t take long-distance flights often, so I am worried about developing this syndrome. Now I am not worried anymore. Thank you doctor.
E6You are welcome, have a safe and happy trip.不用谢，一路平安，旅途愉快。
背景
Economy class syndrome 经济舱并发症: the formation of blood clots (血凝块) in veins (静脉) deep within the legs deep vein thrombosis (深度静脉血栓), occuring during (or just after) a long airplane flight, especially in economy class (tourist class) where there is the least space allotted per passenger and  legs tend especially to be immobilized for lack of leg room.
The economy class syndrome is directly related to immobility for long periods during which blood pools in the legs, raising the risk of clot formation. The tendency to immobility is often compounded by the fasten-seat belt sign, the presence of carts in the aisles, etc.
Other risk factors (风险因素) contributing to the syndrome include lower oxygen pressure and dehydration. Changes in oxygen pressure in the cabin tend to decrease the oxygen level in the blood. The  air in the cabin lacks the normal degree of humidity which contributes to dehydration. The serving of coffee, tea and alcoholic beverages (all of which are diuretics) further causes passengers to become dehydrated.
The economy class syndrome is not confined(受限制) to that class on a plane. It is recommended that all persons traveling on air flights, irrespective of which clss they are in, drink lots of water and move their legs by salking whenever possible and by periodically flexing and extending their ankles, knees, and hips(臀部) to minimize risk of economy class syndrome. Deep Vein Thrombosis is potentially serious and can even have fatal consequences.
重点

entry.title: CCL家庭暴力
entry.content:
A Chinese woman, Mrs. Qian, has been helped by a women’s refuge to obtain a temporary intervention order against her husband because she claims he has been beating her up. She is now living with friends at a secret address, and has gone back to see the lawyer, Dane Gray, to discuss what needs to happen next.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning, The court granted the intervention order last Wednesday. Has your husband tried to contact you since then?19
M1他尝试过。上个周五他送花到我朋友的餐馆，我朋友把花交给我了。He has. Last Friday he sent flowers to my friend’s restaurant and my friend gave me the flowers.
E2He’s not supposed to do that. Once the Apprehended Domestic Violence Order has been served on him, he’s not allowed to make any attempt to contact you. Does he know where you live now?34
M2不知道，要是他知道了，我就永远躲不开他了。可能上个周五他还没收到 法院的信吧。也许警察联系不上他。No, he doesn’t. If he knew, I would never get rid of him. Maybe he hadn’t received the letter from the court last Friday. Maybe the police couldn’t get in touch with him.
E3I’ll find out when he actually received it. If he sent you the flowers after the order had been served on him, he’s in breach of the order and we need to go straight back to court.37
M3你看，我也不想他受到什么惩罚，我只想他离我远点就可以了。你说我们 还要去一次，是为什么？Look, it’s not that I want him to be punished. I just want to keep him away from me. You’re saying we have to go to the court again. What’s that for?
E4The order was just an interim order to keep your husband away until the court could consider the case fully. When we go back next week, we’ll have to present more evidence.33
M4可是上次出庭的时候我把我的地址 – 旧的那个 – 交给法官。要是这次要给 他新的地址，我丈夫就知道我住在哪里了。他会来抓我的。But last time I gave my previous address to the magistrate. If I provide my new one, my husband will know where I live. He will come and get me.
E5Don’t worry. The court will let you write your address on a piece of paper and hand it to the magistrate.21
M5对不起，我总是很紧张，很害怕。可我再也不想被他打了。下次出庭前我 们需要什么证据呢？Sorry I’m always nervous and terrified. I don’t want to be beaten up. What evidence do we need to prepare for the next hearing?
E6Evidence that proves he did actually attack you physically –. Did you ever go to see a doctor after one of these incidents?23
M6我是有这样的证据。昨天我在看我们蜜月的照片，我穿了件无袖上装，两 个胳膊上都能看到瘀青。连我们度蜜月的时候他都打我，你能相信么？I  have this sort of proof. While I was looking at our honeymoon pictures yesterday, I noticed I was wearing a sleeveless top and you can see bruises on my arms. Can you believe he even beat me on our honeymoon?
重点:
